authority: repository-local skill, documentation, policy, test, and validation writes only; forward evaluators were read-only; no provider, workflow, deployment, credential, release, or publication operation

checks.result: The repository-owned dependency-free quick_validate.py accepted docs-maintainer and all three changed PRD skills.
check:skills passed 167 files and 16 mirrors; check:docs passed 206 selected files; bounded detail receipts were written under ignored tmp/.

Forty-two tooling tests include failures for missing ordinary-change and PRD-phase routes, app-owned runbooks, metadata/mirror, portability, background publication controls, tool-authority separation, and undated provider actuality.

checks.check: fresh Effect and service contract scenario
checks.result: A fresh worker selected canonical @bundjil/eve Schemas, errors, WorkspaceOperations, live/memory Layers, tool boundary, selective architecture/README decisions, active SPEC/tasks, and exact focused plus aggregate checks without inventing provider proof.

checks.check: fresh provider runbook and authority scenario
checks.result: A fresh worker treated Executor/Sendblue responses as observations, stopped before consequential operations, routed future procedures to apps/agent/runbooks under HGI-303, authority to HGI-304, and proof to HGI-305.

A fresh worker scoped an attached Claude-workflow edit to repository owners while keeping an unverified README finding in a report-only candidate with reviewer, publisher, quarantine, recovery, and readback requirements.

Effect setup, boundaries, docs/skills, 42 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixture, Knip, five typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with schema-valid synthetic Executor test configuration and no provider call.

limitations: Fresh scenarios prove worker routing and judgment against the candidate checkout; they do not prove provider, workflow, deployment, credential, message, release, or production behavior.
HGI-303, HGI-304, and HGI-305 remain the owners for app runbooks, consequential authority, and critical-journey proof; the skill preserves those gaps rather than implementing them.
Clean-clone validation remains required against the immutable semantic commit before acceptance.
nonClaims: No provider, workflow, GitHub setting, secret, deployment, webhook, message, credential, production, release, or publication system was read or changed.
A tool result, passing local check, or fresh evaluator response does not grant external operation authority.
rollback: Remove the local docs-maintainer skill, mirror, portable validator, PRD/root invocations, HGI-310 policy fixtures, and candidate status updates together while preserving accepted HGI-302 controls.
